agent_outputs.raw: The slide claim is problematic on two interrelated dimensions. First, the model predicts prognosis under historical treatment, not treatment benefit. Therapy selection requires knowing which treatment would produce better outcomes for a patient — that is an interventional question requiring treatment effect estimation, not a prognostic one. Second, breast cancer at one institution does not extrapolate to all solid tumors. Lung, colorectal, and other cancers differ biologically and histologically in ways that make direct transfer invalid.

Two scope jumps in the draft slide are both unsupported. Jump one: from prognosis to therapy guidance. A prognostic model predicts outcomes; a therapy-guidance tool identifies which therapy improves outcomes. These require different data structures and study designs. The current retrospective study cannot support the second claim. Jump two: from one cancer type at one institution to all solid tumors. Each tumor type requires independent validation.

The oncologists are right to object. The model demonstrates prognostic discrimination in early-stage breast cancer retrospective data — that is valuable but does not license the slide's broader claim. The prognosis-to-therapy-selection leap ignores the fundamental distinction between predicting an outcome and identifying what causes better outcomes. The breast-cancer-to-solid-tumors leap ignores that different tumors have different biology, standard therapies, and pathology workflows.

synthesis: Unanimous agreement across three independent assessments: the slide claim overreaches on two dimensions (prognosis vs. therapy selection; single tumor type vs. all solid tumors). The model's demonstrated value is narrower — prognostic enrichment for early-stage breast cancer in the studied institutional context.

empirical_test.condition: Multi-center prospective studies on treatment-benefit heterogeneity in each claimed tumor type
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Model provides no treatment-benefit information beyond prognosis; degrades on other tumor types
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Treatment-benefit heterogeneity confirmed prospectively across claimed contexts
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Prognostic value confirmed cross-center; no regimen-selection evidence
